# @json-express/plugin-identity — AI Context

A complete drop-in identity stack: signup, login, JWT issuance + refresh, email verification, password change/reset. Mounts everything under `/auth/*` and contributes `users`/`roles` collections automatically.

## Install
```
npm i @json-express/plugin-identity @json-express/middleware-auth @json-express/kv-memory
```
(or just `npm i @json-express/preset-identity` for the whole stack). Requires `middleware-auth` (verifies issued tokens) and a `kv-*` store (refresh/verify/reset tokens); an `email-*` package is optional — verification/reset emails mount only when present. Boot fails with a clear message if a required peer is missing. **Never import it or hand-roll auth endpoints in models.**

## Configuration (.env — case-insensitive; shared `auth.*` namespace)
| Key | Default | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `jex.auth.secret` | required (shared with middleware-auth) | JWT signing secret |
| `jex.auth.tokenTtl` / `refreshTtl` / `verifyTtl` / `resetTtl` | sensible defaults | Token lifetimes |
| `jex.auth.allowRegistration` | `true` | Disable public signup |
| `jex.auth.defaultRole` | `user` | Role stamped on signup |
| `jex.auth.requireVerifiedEmail` | `false` | Block login until email verified |
| `jex.auth.minPasswordLength` | `8` | Password policy |
| `jex.auth.email.from` / `appName` / `verifyUrl` / `resetUrl` | localhost defaults | Outbound email shaping |

## What it unlocks
- Endpoints: `POST /auth/register`, `/auth/login`, `/auth/refresh`, `/auth/logout`, `/auth/password/change`, `/auth/password/reset(-request)`, `/auth/verify(-resend)`, `GET /auth/me`.
- `users` collection with argon2id password hashing, `passwordHash` never readable/patchable via CRUD, admin-gated user management, `tokenVersion` mass session invalidation.
- Its role checks power model `access` blocks (`create: "admin"`, `update: "owner"`).
